steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Triangle ABC has a right angle at C with $AC = 8$ and $AB = \sqrt{89}$. Triangle ADE has a right angle at E with $AE = AC + CE = 8 + 24 = 32$. Points A, B, D are collinear (D lies on the extension of AB beyond B). Find BC first, then use the shared slope to get DE, then compute the area of triangle ADE.

steps.title: Find BC
steps.content: By the Pythagorean theorem in triangle ABC: $BC^2 + AC^2 = AB^2$, so $BC^2 + 64 = 89$, giving $BC = 5$.

steps.title: Find DE
steps.content: Since A, B, D are collinear, the slope from A is $\frac{BC}{AC} = \frac{5}{8}$. Point E is 32 units horizontally from A and D is directly above E, so $DE = \frac{5}{8} \times 32 = 20$.
